title: Infosys to acquire 75% stake in Versent Group from Telstra
detailed_summary: Infosys announced a joint venture with Telstra in Australia under which Infosys Singapore Pte. Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys Limited, will acquire 75% of the equity share capital in Telstra Purple Pty Ltd, referred to as Versent Group. Telstra will retain a 25% minority stake, while Infosys will have operational control. The cash consideration is AUD 233.25 million for the 75% stake, including upfront and deferred consideration, including earnouts, and excluding management incentives and retention bonus. Versent Group reported FY25 revenues of AUD 211.4 million for the fiscal year ending June 30th and has 650 engineers, advisors, and strategists across Australia. The transaction is expected to close during the second half of fiscal year 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.

key_facts.fact: Infosys will acquire 75% of the shareholding in Versent Group, Australia's leading Digital Transformation Solutions Provider and a wholly owned subsidiary of Telstra Group.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys and Telstra Announce Joint Venture in Australia

key_facts.fact: Infosys will have operational control, while Telstra will continue to retain a 25% minority stake in Versent Group.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys and Telstra Announce Joint Venture in Australia

key_facts.fact: The acquisition will be made by Infosys Singapore Pte Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys Limited.
The cost of acquisition is AUD 233.25 million for 75% stake, including upfront, and deferred consideration, including earnouts; excluding management incentives, and retention bonus.
Versent Group reported FY25 revenues of AUD 211.4 million for the fiscal year ending June 30th.
The transaction is expected to close during second half of fiscal year 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.
Governmental or regulatory approvals are required from the Foreign Investment Review Board of Australia and the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission.
